_about: The actual wholesale cost of tenant screening, used to compute the markup a landlord adds on top of an application fee. Prices are the landlord/property-side cost of a full screen (credit + criminal + eviction) and move over time — verify before relying on a specific number.

actual_cost.summary: A full tenant screen (credit + national criminal + eviction history) wholesale costs a landlord roughly $18–$45 — about $30 in the typical case. A credit-only or background-only check is often $15–$30.

typical_charged.summary: Landlords and property managers typically charge applicants $40–$75 (often $50), and some charge $100–$150 — well above what the screen costs them.

vendors.name: TransUnion SmartMove
vendors.note: Often billed to the applicant; landlord pays ~$25–$40 per full report.

vendors.name: RentPrep
vendors.note: Tiered packages; basic credit/background at the low end.

vendors.name: Avail / Zillow / Apartments.com tools
vendors.note: Many listing platforms bundle screening; some are free to the landlord, with the applicant charged a flat $35–$55.

context: Many large platforms (Zillow, Apartments.com, Avail) let an applicant pay one screening fee and REUSE the report across multiple listings within 30 days — so paying a fresh fee at every property is often unnecessary.
When a landlord charges every applicant a $50–$75 fee but only screens the one they pick, the fees collected from rejected applicants are pure margin.
